title: 'An Iranian diplomatic victory over Trump would be celebrated amid ruins'
subhed: While Iran's leadership has demonstrated its capacity for survival, as evidenced by the rise of new leaders as uncompromising as their predecessors, the consequences of the war launched on February 28 are likely to be dire for a regime already on the brink, writes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Malian jihadists and rebels ramp up pressure on junta: 'We are blocking Bamako'
subhed: Jihadists from the Al Qaeda-affiliated group JNIM announced a total siege of the Malian capital on Tuesday. At least five towns in the country's north have fallen to the attackers. 'The situation is under control,' President Assimi Goita said.

title: Chanel's Matthieu Blazy brings a breath of fresh air to Biarritz
subhed: The artistic director unveiled his first cruise collection on Tuesday, April 28, in the Basque Country city where the house's signature style was born. The show was all elegance and lightness.

title: Moldovan President Maia Sandu: 'For us, joining the EU is not a dream but a survival strategy as a democratic state'
subhed: In an interview with Le Monde, President Maia Sandu advocates for the European integration of Moldova, Ukraine and the Western Balkans to withstand Russian pressure. She also raises the possibility of unifying her country with Romania to speed up the process.

title: How Vietnam's garment factories benefited from Trump's trade policy
